description: Add DynamoDB metadata table and integrate it with the Step Functions state machine using TDD workflow. Write failing CDK assertion tests first, then implement the DynamoDB table construct (SleepAudioMetadataTable) with on-demand billing, encryption, and point-in-time recovery. Extend the state machine to include DynamoDB PutItem/UpdateItem tasks for writing initial metadata records and updating status. Update EventBridge input transformer to pass full S3 event data. Finally, update ARCHITECTURE.md with the new DynamoDB metadata layer.
steps: Step 1 - Create a new test file src/test/java/com/myorg/DynamoDbMetadataTest.java. Write failing tests that assert: (a) a DynamoDB table resource exists with partition key 'audioId' (type S), (b) billing mode is PAY_PER_REQUEST, (c) server-side encryption is enabled (AWS_OWNED), (d) point-in-time recovery is enabled, (e) removal policy is RETAIN. Follow the same @BeforeEach / Template.fromStack pattern used in CdkBaseTest.java and StepFunctionsTest.java.

Step 2 - Add additional failing tests in DynamoDbMetadataTest.java that verify state machine integration: (a) the synthesized template JSON contains 'dynamodb:PutItem' (indicating a DynamoDB PutItem task in the state machine definition), (b) an IAM policy exists granting dynamodb:PutItem and dynamodb:UpdateItem to the state machine role scoped to the table, (c) the state machine definition contains 'dynamodb:UpdateItem' for status updates.

Step 3 - Implement the DynamoDB table in src/main/java/com/myorg/CdkBaseStack.java: Create a Table construct with id 'SleepAudioMetadataTable', partition key 'audioId' (STRING), billing mode PAY_PER_REQUEST, encryption TABLE_DEFAULT (AWS owned), point-in-time recovery enabled, removal policy RETAIN. Import software.amazon.awscdk.services.dynamodb.Table, TableEncryption, BillingMode, Attribute, AttributeType.

Step 4 - Extend the Step Functions state machine definition in CdkBaseStack.java: (a) Add a DynamoDB PutItem task (using CallAwsService with service='dynamodb' action='putItem') as the FIRST step in the chain, BEFORE the existing Polly task. This task writes an initial metadata record with fields: audioId (from S3 object key via JsonPath), status='PROCESSING', inputBucket (from S3 event), inputKey (from S3 event), createdAt (from context timestamp). (b) Add a DynamoDB UpdateItem task AFTER the Polly task that sets status='COMPLETED' and updatedAt. (c) Chain them: PutItem -> Polly -> UpdateItem. Use the table name via table.getTableName() in the parameters.
Step 5 - Grant the state machine role permissions to read/write the DynamoDB table. Use table.grantReadWriteData(stateMachine) which applies least-privilege IAM policy.
Step 6 - Run 'mvn test' to confirm all new and existing tests pass (10 old + new DynamoDB tests).

Step 7 - Update ARCHITECTURE.md: (a) In the Implementation Status table, add a row for 'DynamoDB Metadata Table' with logical ID 'SleepAudioMetadataTable' and configuration details (PAY_PER_REQUEST, encryption, PITR). (b) Update the Orchestration Layer section to describe the new state machine flow: PutItem (PROCESSING) -> Polly SynthesizeSpeech -> UpdateItem (COMPLETED). (c) In the Mermaid diagram, change DDB from 'planned' class to 'implemented' class, and add data flow arrows from SFN to DDB. (d) Move DynamoDB Table from the 'Planned' table to the 'Implemented' table.
Step 8 - Run 'mvn test' one final time to confirm everything is green after ARCHITECTURE.md changes (no code impact but good practice).
acceptance_criteria: A new test file src/test/java/com/myorg/DynamoDbMetadataTest.java exists with at least 5 test methods
Running 'mvn test' passes all tests including the new DynamoDB tests
The synthesized CloudFormation template includes an AWS::DynamoDB::Table resource with KeySchema containing audioId as HASH key
The DynamoDB table uses PAY_PER_REQUEST billing mode
The DynamoDB table has PointInTimeRecoverySpecification enabled
The state machine definition includes DynamoDB PutItem and UpdateItem tasks (verified by template JSON containing 'dynamodb:PutItem' and 'dynamodb:UpdateItem')
An IAM policy grants dynamodb:PutItem, dynamodb:UpdateItem permissions to the state machine execution role
ARCHITECTURE.md Mermaid diagram shows DDB with 'implemented' styling
ARCHITECTURE.md Implementation Status table includes DynamoDB Metadata Table
verification: Run 'mvn test' and confirm all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Run 'mvn compile' and confirm clean compilation with no errors
